pr.title: Melee decomp: Interface and trophy UI matches
pr.author: fjooord

summary: Implementation-only decompilation match slice for Interface text/HUD helpers and TY Figupon. The PR touched four C files, claimed 7 newly exact functions across the slice, and included verification claims of zero broken matches, fuzzy regressions, or metric regressions. The bot report in the loaded corpus lists 8 new matches and 4 unmatched-item improvements, creating a small count mismatch with the PR body.

key_files.role: Largest matching and cleanup target; changes DevText_Create, DevText_Print, cursor clamp code, color/global references, menu movement locals, and coordinate cast structure.
evidence_refs: diff: textlib.c hunks for DevText_Create, DevText_SetCursorXY, DevText_Print, un_80302FFC, un_80303AC4, un_80304210
text_corpus: decomp-dev bot report lists DevText_Print and DevText_Create as new matches plus textlib unmatched improvements

key_files.role: HUD/interface struct layout and matching cleanup; refactors un_804A1F58 from array-of-wrapper shape to one struct containing a six-entry x8 array, updates accessors and sizeof memzero.
evidence_refs: diff: ifcoget.c struct un_804A1F58_t and un_804A1F58_x8_t changes
diff: ifcoget.c fn_802FF218, un_802FF364, un_802FF498, un_802FF4FC, un_802FF570, un_802FF620
text_corpus: bot report lists .bss, un_802FF570, un_802FF620 as new matches and fn_802FF218 as improved

key_files.role: Small loop/local-shape matching update in stock HUD helper fn_802FA6C4.
evidence_refs: diff: ifstock.c fn_802FA6C4 loop variable split, pointer increments, and reverse cleanup loop
text_corpus: bot report lists fn_802FA6C4 as a new match

key_files.role: Small local-shape and naming update in trophy Figupon functions.
evidence_refs: diff: tyfigupon.c tyFigupon_80314C5C temporary JObj local and fn_803155C8 bet_count local
text_corpus: bot report lists tyFigupon_80314C5C as a new match and fn_803155C8 as improved

what_changed.text: Matched IF text/HUD and TY Figupon functions without header churn according to the PR body.

In ifcoget.c, recovered a more specific un_804A1F58 layout: one top-level object with pad_x4 and x8[6], adding xC and x10 fields to each x8 entry and replacing a hard-coded 0x80 memzero with sizeof(un_804A1F58).
evidence_refs: diff: ifcoget.c struct and un_802FF498 hunks

what_changed.text: In textlib.c, DevText_Create was adjusted to use existing symbol-map/literal-pool objects for default background/text colors and scale constants, and to reference canonical un_804D6E18/un_804D6E38 pool/list symbols.
In textlib.c, DevText_Print replaced byte-indexed buffer writes with a local DevTextGlyph typed view containing chr and color bitfields.
evidence_refs: diff: textlib.c DevTextGlyph typedef and DevText_Print hunk

what_changed.text: In ifstock.c, fn_802FA6C4 changed loop locals and pointer iteration to match codegen, including q/w pointer increments, a p pointer based at ifStock_804A1774.x0, and a separate reverse-loop variable j.
In tyfigupon.c, local variables were reshaped: GET_JOBJ result is staged through tmp_jobj, and an x5D-derived value was renamed/handled as bet_count in fn_803155C8.
evidence_refs: diff: tyfigupon.c tyFigupon_80314C5C and fn_803155C8 hunks

smart_moves.text: Replacing the inaccurate TODO/hard-coded size in ifcoget.c with a struct layout that allows sizeof(un_804A1F58) made the cleanup both more typed and less magic-number dependent.
evidence_refs: diff: ifcoget.c removal of TODO and memzero(&un_804A1F58, sizeof(un_804A1F58))

smart_moves.text: The PR documented why apparently new externs in textlib.c were existing section-owned objects rather than new data state, reducing review risk around data-symbol churn.
Using a narrow typed DevTextGlyph overlay made DevText_Print express the packed glyph/color buffer more directly than manual byte offset writes.
evidence_refs: diff: textlib.c DevTextGlyph typedef and DevText_Print hunk

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a repeated array wrapper shape produces awkward indexing, check whether the binary layout is a single owner struct containing an inner fixed-size array. This can enable real sizeof cleanup and clearer field ownership.
evidence_refs: diff: ifcoget.c un_804A1F58 changed from un_804A1F58[7] to one un_804A1F58 with x8[6]
diff: ifcoget.c memzero changed from hard-coded 0x80 to sizeof(un_804A1F58)

decomp_lessons.lesson: For packed UI buffers, a small local struct or bitfield overlay can be a cleaner match hypothesis than manual byte indexing when the access pattern is semantically one record per cell.
evidence_refs: diff: textlib.c DevText_Print writes chr and color through ((DevTextGlyph*) text->buf)[index]

decomp_lessons.lesson: If exact matching requires referencing literal-pool or shared data symbols, document section ownership and prior existence so reviewers can distinguish necessary data references from speculative new extern state.

naming_conventions.text: The PR favored canonical address-style symbol-map names such as un_804D6E18, un_804D6E38, un_804D5A08, and un_804DDC88 when semantic friendly names were not shared or fully established across TUs.
evidence_refs: diff: textlib.c extern and GXColor rename hunks

naming_conventions.text: Local variable naming was made more semantic where evidence was local and narrow, e.g. sc became bet_count in fn_803155C8.

The PR introduced descriptive temporary/role names only locally, such as DevTextGlyph, cur, p, j, k, thing, tmp_jobj, and bet_count; it did not claim broader symbol renames beyond shown file-local/global data changes.
evidence_refs: diff: textlib.c, ifstock.c, ifcoget.c, tyfigupon.c hunks

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was retained or adjusted in several functions to preserve stack/codegen shape.
evidence_refs: diff: ifcoget.c fn_802FF218 PAD_STACK(24), un_802FF364 PAD_STACK(0x10), un_802FF498 PAD_STACK(8)

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: A narrow #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop block was added around DevText_SetCursorXY while expanding clamp logic inline instead of calling DevText_Clamp twice.
Local lifetime and register/stack shaping tactics were used, including temporary locals, separate loop counters, explicit casts, scoped blocks, and no-op casts such as (void) j and (void) i.
evidence_refs: diff: ifstock.c fn_802FA6C4 adds p, j, k
diff: textlib.c un_80303AC4 uses u8 j, int i, (void) j, (void) i
diff: textlib.c un_80304210 introduces f32 x, scoped s32 x_pos/y_pos

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Data references in DevText_Create were shifted from inline literals and friendly names to existing address-style symbols to close the exact match.

review_feedback.text: A textlib.c concern about extern-looking additions was answered by the PR author: the additions were described as existing .sdata2 literal-pool objects needed for DevText_Create and existing shared .sbss/.sdata objects referenced by canonical symbol-map names.
The author specifically stated that un_804DDC88 through un_804DDCA0 already existed as .sdata2 objects before the PR, and that keeping inline/local literals left DevText_Create just short of exact.

The author stated that un_804D5A08 through un_804D5A14 were already real .sdata objects and that the change was an address-name rename, with possible future cleanup to friendlier aliases once surrounding functions are better understood.

The author noted that un_804D6E18 and un_804D6E38 correspond to draw-list and pool-head state that textdraw.c locally aliases as devtext_drawlist and devtext_poolhead; any friendlier naming should be shared rather than allowing cross-TU extern-name drift.
A review by jurrejelle approved the PR, but the loaded evidence contains no written approval rationale.
evidence_refs: text_corpus: review by jurrejelle state APPROVED with empty body

follow_up_queries: textlib.c DevText_Create un_804DDC88 un_804DDCA0 literal-pool exact match
textlib.c devtext_drawlist devtext_poolhead un_804D6E18 un_804D6E38 shared alias
ifstock fn_802FA6C4 q w pointer increment p[1] == -2

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, dates, changed file counts
human_pr_text: summary, scope, verification commands and regression claims
raw_diff: complete 16250-byte diff excerpt loaded untruncated
text_corpus: bot report, review comments, approval state
notes: The loaded raw diff and extracted comments are sufficient to ground changed files, tactics, naming discussion, and review feedback.

There is a mismatch between the PR body claim of 7 newly exact functions and the decomp-dev bot report listing 8 new matches, including a .bss item; this intake records both without resolving the discrepancy.

Most detailed review feedback in the loaded slice is authored by fjooord, the PR author, apparently responding to a concern; only an empty approval by jurrejelle is loaded as external reviewer feedback.
Verification results are reported in the PR body and bot comment; this intake did not rerun tools and does not independently validate them.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover owner struct plus inner array before keeping awkward wrapper-array indexing
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2691 supports a PR-scoped lesson from ifcoget.c: changing un_804A1F58 from an array of wrapper structs to a single owner struct with x8[6] enabled clearer field access and replacement of a hard-coded memzero size with sizeof(un_804A1F58).
evidence_refs: diff: ifcoget.c un_802FF498 memzero sizeof change

accepted_candidate_records.title: Typed overlay can clarify packed text buffers
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2691 supports a PR-scoped lesson from textlib.c: a narrow DevTextGlyph overlay with a character byte and color bitfield replaced manual two-byte indexing in DevText_Print and corresponded to an exact match in the bot report.
evidence_refs: diff: textlib.c DevTextGlyph and DevText_Print hunk

accepted_candidate_records.title: Document section ownership for data-symbol references introduced during code matching
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2691 supports a PR-scoped lesson from textlib.c: when exact matching required address-style references to .sdata2, .sdata, and .sbss objects, the author documented that the objects already existed and explained why the references were not new data state.

source_update_candidates.title: textlib.c shares DevText draw-list and pool-head state with textdraw.c
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2691 review comments state that un_804D6E18 and un_804D6E38 are the same draw-list and pool-head state that textdraw.c locally aliases as devtext_drawlist and devtext_poolhead. Curator should verify against current symbols/source before recording as a path fact.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potentially useful cross-TU data ownership/naming fact, but the PR comment alone should not be promoted without source/symbol verification.

source_update_candidates.title: DevText_Create uses existing .sdata2 literal-pool symbols for default colors and scale constants
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2691 review comments state that un_804DDC88 through un_804DDCA0 were existing .sdata2 objects before the PR and were needed for exact DevText_Create matching of default background/text colors and 10.0f/16.0f. Curator should verify symbol ownership and current source before recording.
evidence_refs: diff: textlib.c DevText_Create references un_804DDC88, un_804DDC8C, un_804DDC90, un_804DDC94, un_804DDC98, un_804DDC9C, un_804DDCA0
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful data ownership detail, but global/path facts require current symbol/source confirmation.

source_update_candidates.title: textlib.c color globals at 804D5A08-804D5A14 may be canonical address-named .sdata objects
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2691 review comments state that un_804D5A08 through un_804D5A14 were existing .sdata objects and that the PR renamed color_08/color_0C/color_10/color_14 to address-style names to match symbols.txt. Curator should verify current naming and symbol ownership.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential data-symbol naming/path fact; needs current source verification and should not override conservative naming standards without evidence.

source_update_candidates.title: Consider shared friendly aliases for DevText draw-list and pool-head symbols
source_update_candidates.text: The PR author suggested that if friendlier names are desired for un_804D6E18/un_804D6E38 and textdraw.c aliases, they should be shared rather than separate extern names drifting from the symbol map.
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a cleanup idea from review discussion, not an accepted naming standard or current path fact.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize this PR into a rule that address-style data names should replace friendly names; the loaded evidence supports only this textlib.c match context and explicitly mentions possible future friendlier shared aliases.

Do not treat pragma dont_inline or PAD_STACK use as generally acceptable style from this PR alone; standards require narrow verifier evidence, and the PR provides high-level match/regression reports but no function-by-function pragma justification beyond the diff.
Do not independently claim the exact set of matched functions as 7 or 8 without preserving the PR-body versus bot-report discrepancy.
